description:
You are acting as a Site Reliability Engineer who has just received a small sample of raw uptime data in CSV format.  
Your job is to create a concise incident-readiness report consisting of two derivative files that will later be shipped to the on-call dashboard.

There is exactly one input file:

• /home/user/data/uptime_sample.csv – A comma-separated file that already exists on disk.  
  (Do NOT rename or alter this source file. Work only on copies/derivatives in the new directory described below.)

Required final state (all paths are absolute):

1. Directory  
   /home/user/sre_reports  
   • Must exist when you are done.  
   • Must contain only the three files listed below (nothing else).

2. /home/user/sre_reports/downtime_events.csv  
   • CSV, UTF-8, Unix line endings (LF only).  
   • First line (header) must be:  
     timestamp,service,status  
   • Must contain ONLY the rows from the original uptime_sample.csv where the status column equals the literal string “DOWN”.  
   • Row order must be preserved exactly as it appeared in the source file.  
   • There must be a final newline (line-feed) after the last row.

3. /home/user/sre_reports/downtime_summary.json  
   • JSON object, pretty-printed with 2-space indentation.  
   • Keys are the distinct service names found in the downtime_events.csv file, sorted lexicographically (ASCIIbetical).  
   • Values are integers representing the total number of DOWN minutes for that service (each row in the input represents one minute).  
   • The file must end with exactly one trailing newline.  
   • Example format (do not rely on the exact numbers here—your script must calculate them from the data):  
     {  
       "api-gateway": 42,  
       "auth-service": 17  
     }

4. /home/user/sre_reports/action_log.txt  
   • Plain-text log of the high-level shell commands or one-sentence descriptions you executed or scripted, one line per entry, in the order you performed them.  
   • A minimum of 5 and a maximum of 10 non-empty lines.  
   • Each line must begin with an ISO-8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) followed by a single space and then your note.  
   • Example (illustrative only):  
     2023-09-15T08:45:01Z Created sre_reports directory

Constraints & hints:

• Work entirely with standard command-line tools available on an up-to-date Debian/Ubuntu environment (e.g., mkdir, awk, grep, cut, jq, etc.).  
• You do NOT have sudo access.  
• Ensure file permissions allow read/write for the current user (default umask is fine).  
• Keep everything idempotent so the task can be re-run without manual cleanup.

The automated tests will confirm that:
• The directory /home/user/sre_reports exists.  
• downtime_events.csv exactly matches the specification (header, correct rows, correct order, correct newline style).  
• downtime_summary.json is syntactically valid, 2-space indented, keys sorted, correct counts, and ends with a single newline.  
• action_log.txt contains 5–10 well-formed timestamped lines.

Produce no additional files anywhere else on the filesystem.
truth:
Pre-existing file before task start:

Path: /home/user/data/uptime_sample.csv  
Contents (LF line endings):

timestamp,service,status
2023-09-01T00:00:00Z,auth-service,UP
2023-09-01T00:01:00Z,auth-service,DOWN
2023-09-01T00:02:00Z,auth-service,UP
2023-09-01T00:00:00Z,api-gateway,UP
2023-09-01T00:01:00Z,api-gateway,UP
2023-09-01T00:02:00Z,api-gateway,DOWN
2023-09-01T00:03:00Z,api-gateway,DOWN
2023-09-01T00:04:00Z,api-gateway,UP

Expected files after successful completion:

1. /home/user/sre_reports/downtime_events.csv  
Exact contents:

timestamp,service,status
2023-09-01T00:01:00Z,auth-service,DOWN
2023-09-01T00:02:00Z,api-gateway,DOWN
2023-09-01T00:03:00Z,api-gateway,DOWN

2. /home/user/sre_reports/downtime_summary.json  
Exact contents (with trailing LF):

{
  "api-gateway": 2,
  "auth-service": 1
}

3. /home/user/sre_reports/action_log.txt  
• 5–10 lines, each starting with a valid ISO-8601 UTC timestamp then a space, followed by some free-text description.  
• The exact text is not checked, only the format and line count are enforced.

No other files should be present inside /home/user/sre_reports.
